product_updates.title: How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
product_updates.content: GTC — which stands for GPU Technology Conference — is Nvidia's flagship annual event, where the chipmaker typically uses the spotlight to announce new products, champion partnerships, and lay out its vision for the future of computing. Huang's keynote will focus on Nvidia's role in the future of …

product_updates.title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
product_updates.content: Rox, founded in 2024 by the former chief growth officer of New Relic, offers an AI-native alternative to CRM tools.

product_updates.title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
product_updates.content: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup location, and price.

product_updates.title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
product_updates.content: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to reengage its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

product_updates.title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
product_updates.content: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

product_updates.title: Bumble introduces an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
product_updates.content: Bumble's new AI assistant Bee will move the dating app beyond the swipe by matching people based on compatibility and goals.

product_updates.title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
product_updates.content: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for violating her privacy and publicity rights.

product_updates.title: Gumloop lands $50M from Benchmark to turn every employee into an AI agent builder
product_updates.content: As companies race to adopt AI, Benchmark general partner Everett Randle believes the key to success lies in empowering every worker with AI superpowers, and Gumloop’s intuitive agent builder is an example of the kind of tool that will unlock that potential.

product_updates.title: Alexa+ gets a new ‘adults only’ personality option that curses but won’t do NSFW content
product_updates.content: The new Sassy style can curse and roast you, but the fun ends there.

product_updates.title: Wonderful raises $150M Series B at $2B valuation
product_updates.content: The funding round, led by Insight Partners, comes just four months after Wonderful raised a $100 million Series A.

technical_breakthroughs.title: A defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
technical_breakthroughs.content: The US military might use generative AI systems to rank lists of targets and make recommendations—which would be vetted by humans—about which to strike first, according to a Defense Department official with knowledge of the matter. The disclosure about how the military may use AI chatbots comes a…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The who, what, and why of the attack that has shut down Stryker's Windows network
technical_breakthroughs.content: Company says it doesn't know how long it will take to restore its Microsoft environment.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: Early adopters cash in on China’s OpenClaw craze, and US batteries slump
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
technical_breakthroughs.content: The impact of artificial intelligence extends far beyond the digital world and into our everyday lives, across the cars we drive, the appliances in our homes, and medical devices that keep people alive. technical_breakthroughs.title: Brutal times for the US battery industry
technical_breakthroughs.content: Just a few years ago, the battery industry was hot, hot, hot. There was a seemingly infinite number of companies popping up, with shiny new chemistries and massive fundraising rounds.
